summary: reconcileStreetsAction runs position updates/inserts before deleting removed rows, so any street-list edit that isn't a pure append transiently duplicates a (route_id, position) pair and violates the non-deferrable UNIQUE constraint route_street_route_id_position_key (344.sql), aborting the transaction.
failure_scenario: Save a route [A]; load it and reverse it (or insert/remove a mid-route street) and click 'Update route' → PUT /userapi/routes/:id: first UPDATE/INSERT sets position 0 while the old row still occupies position 0 → PSQLException 23505; withSlugRetry re-runs and fails again → 500, user sees generic save-error toast and the edit is lost. The spec's own 'replace the street list in place' test exercises exactly this.

summary: The PR adds a 17th field (userRouteId) to the Mission case class but does not update this pre-existing spec's Mission(...) constructor call, so sbt test fails to compile ('Unspecified value parameter userRouteId').
failure_scenario: Run sbt test (or CI's DB-backed test job): test compilation errors at ExploreAddressServiceSpec.scala:544, so the entire suite — including the new RouteBuilderControllerSpec — cannot execute. Verified by running testOnly in the web container.

summary: The Start address field's handler appends the chosen point to the END of the route (onSetStart just calls #addWaypoint, which pushes onto #waypoints), so once any waypoint exists, typing a new Start address extends the route instead of setting/moving its start.
failure_scenario: Build a route with 2+ clicks, then type a different address into the Start field: the geocoded point is routed from the current END and appended, so the route's tail walks toward the typed 'start' while the actual start flag never moves.

summary: For a route whose total street distance is 0 (e.g. a route saved with an empty streets array, which POST /saveRoute accepts unvalidated), the route-scoped mission branch returns None and the .map(_.get) throws NoSuchElementException.
failure_scenario: POST /saveRoute with {"region_id": r, "streets": []} (accepted, returns route_id) then open /explore?routeId=<id> (or /r/<slug>): getRouteDistance == 0 → DBIO.successful(None) → .get throws inside the transaction → Explore page 500s for anyone using that link.

summary: getUsageCounts counts user_route rows, not distinct users, so the 'Explored by {n} · Completed by {m}' card stats double-count a person who walks the route more than once.
failure_scenario: A user completes a route (user_route.completed = true), then opens the same route link again — getActiveRouteOrCreateNew only reuses rows with !completed && !discarded, so a second user_route row is created; cards report 'Explored by 2 · Completed by 1' though only one person ever walked it.

summary: #reconstructPath decides a street's traversal direction by testing whether the entry node is within 15 m (NODE_TOLERANCE_M * 1.5) of coords[0], so for streets ~10-15 m long the far-end entry node can also fall inside that radius and flip is computed wrong.
failure_scenario: A 12 m connector street entered from its far end: the merged entry node can sit ≤10 m from that endpoint, hence ≤~15 m from coords[0], so enteredAtFirst is true and flip=false though the street is walked end→start — arrows render backwards and the wrong reverse flag is saved to route_street, making Explore start that street from the wrong end.
